title: Waltham animal control officers recover ‘pile ’o pigs’ dumped in woods, cemetery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Waltham Animal Control officers recovered 19 guinea pigs dumped in a cemetery and nearby woods.
The guinea pigs were likely abandoned by breeders due to stricter regulations and decreased pet store purchases.
The Guinea Pig Sanctuary in Salisbury aids in finding homes for rescued guinea pigs, now housing over 260.

title: What Drake Maye, Milton Williams said of A.J. Brown’s potential arrival in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots linked to trade talks with Eagles for star receiver A.J. Brown, pending post-June 1 cap considerations.
Milton Williams and Drake Maye expressed support for Brown, highlighting his potential impact on the Patriots.
Brown recorded 1,003 yards and seven TDs in 2026, while rumors of trade persist among Patriots players.

title: Something fishy? Large groups of dead fish spotted in Boston, Worcester ponds
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dead fish have been seen at Leverett Pond, the Charles River Esplanade, and University Park in Boston and Worcester.
Officials attribute the fish kills to natural causes like low oxygen levels due to warm temperatures and spawning stress.
Massachusetts Environmental Police, MassWildlife, and park rangers are monitoring the situation, but there's no public safety concern.

title: N.H. man killed his mother in murder-suicide, officials say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eileen Shevelin, 78, was asphyxiated by her son Thomas Wallace, 57, in a Dover, N.H. murder-suicide.
Wallace died by suicide with a gunshot to the head after killing his mother, autopsy results confirmed.
Authorities discovered their bodies on May 26 during a welfare check; the family has requested privacy.

title: East Boston couple facing civil rights charges after alleged racist attack on group dining at restaurant
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Arielle and Gregory Raso face civil rights charges for an alleged racist attack on diners at Tertulias Cafe.
The couple allegedly threw wood, sprayed water, and used racial slurs against a Hispanic group, injuring a child.
The Rasos, released on $750 bail each, are ordered to avoid the victims and return to court on July 28.

title: What Craig Breslow said about the Red Sox potentially selling off pieces amid continued struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox chief Craig Breslow says the team is not planning to sell off players despite their poor record.
Boston struggles with inconsistent offense, ranking last in MLB for runs scored with 220 this season.
The team is looking to improve by boosting current player performance and possibly acquiring new talent.

title: Fall River police looking for man accused of destroying shooting victim’s memorial
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fall River police are searching for William Acosta Abreu, 20, accused of vandalizing a memorial for a 2023 murder victim.
Acosta Abreu was filmed destroying the memorial for Diamonte Odom, who was killed by gun violence in May 2023.
Police report several warrants for Acosta Abreu's arrest and seek public assistance in locating him, urging anonymous tips.

title: 5 takeaways from Patriots’ first 2026 OTA practice
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye completed 13-of-22 passes with one interception during the Patriots' first 2026 OTA practice.
Cornerback Christian Gonzalez and wide receiver Kayshon Boutte were absent from the Patriots' OTA session.
Rookie Caleb Lomu participated in drills as right tackle, stepping in for veteran Morgan Moses.

title: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Woody’s Grill & Tap on Hemenway Street will close permanently on Thursday after decades of service.
The restaurant is known for its wood-fired pizza, beer, and hosting community events like art exhibits and poetry readings.
Located near Fenway Park, Woody's Grill & Tap has been a beloved spot for locals and Northeastern University students.

title: A royal family dined at a popular Somerville restaurant this week
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Belgium's Princess Elisabeth dined at Somerville's Sarma Restaurant before her Harvard Kennedy School graduation.
King Philippe and Queen Mathilde were spotted, but Sarma staff couldn't confirm specific family members or their meals.
Princess Elisabeth graduated with a master's in public policy and plans a post-graduation sailing trip across the Atlantic.
